scope: Adversarial review records for high-confidence synthesis propositions in ARGUMENT_GRAPH.json
governing_rules: A review state records epistemic completion, not rhetorical confidence.
Completed milestones are tracked independently from the ordered current_state so experiment design can precede source grounding without falsely implying review completion.
No proposition may be marked source_grounded without at least one verified or partially verified adversarial evidence edge with a pinpoint locator.
No proposition may be marked survives_review unless it is source_grounded, has a discriminating test, records a forced concession, and states the surviving narrowed claim.
An adversarial review does not establish that a current AI system is conscious or nonconscious.

reviews.strongest_countermodel: Major theories may be partially compatible descriptions of shared latent computational or dynamical variables at different levels of analysis, allowing principled evidence pooling through an explicit bridge model.
critic_inferential_route: Different theories can operationalize overlapping phenomena using different vocabularies.
Measures associated with those theories can load on common latent factors.
Cross-theory convergence can therefore contain information not captured by isolated theory-specific models.
A blanket prohibition on pooling could preserve artificial fragmentation and discard predictive convergence.
reviews.forced_concession: Pooling is permissible when a bridge model states the shared variable, transport assumptions, theory-specific residuals, and failure conditions. The target is unlicensed pooling, not pooling in principle.
reviews.confidence_lowering_test: A preregistered bridge model that predicts intervention outcomes better than theory-specific alternatives while remaining calibrated across tasks and species would weaken the claim that operational separation is always required.
reviews.surviving_narrowed_claim: Indicators from rival theories should not be pooled unless an explicit bridge model preserves their distinct commitments and residual disagreements.
reviews.required_next_evidence: A canonical or primary source defending cross-theory unification, common-factor modeling, or principled evidence pooling, represented by an opposing or scope-restricting graph edge with a pinpoint locator.

reviews.strongest_countermodel: Predictively valid biomarkers can support bounded classification before their complete causal role is known, so unresolved constitution or mechanism need not block every cross-substrate inference.
critic_inferential_route: Predictive validity and causal interpretation are distinct achievements.
Science and medicine often use calibrated markers before a full mechanism is established.
AI assessment could use such markers defeasibly while remaining agnostic about constitution.
Therefore role specification is mandatory for strong metaphysical inference but not necessarily for every bounded evidential use.
reviews.forced_concession: The book must distinguish calibrated use of a correlate as defeasible evidence from treating it as a substrate-independent constitutive criterion. The former can be legitimate under demonstrated transport and calibration assumptions.
reviews.confidence_lowering_test: A marker that remains calibrated across neural interventions, species, architectures, and adversarial controls would weaken the claim that unresolved causal role blocks useful cross-substrate inference.
reviews.surviving_narrowed_claim: Any translation of a consciousness-associated marker to AI must state whether it is being used predictively, mechanistically, constitutively, or merely as contextual evidence, and must justify the relevant transport assumptions.
reviews.required_next_evidence: A primary or canonical measurement-theory, biomarker, or no-report source defending calibrated inference under incomplete causal interpretation, encoded as a pinpointed opposing edge.

reviews.strongest_countermodel: Mechanisms can be multiply realized, and theory terms may denote abstract causal roles that an engineered system can instantiate without biological duplication.
critic_inferential_route: Scientific mechanism attribution can operate at an abstract causal-organizational level.
Abstract causal roles may admit multiple physical realizations.
Artificial components can satisfy those roles under intervention even when their material implementation differs from nervous tissue.
Rejecting them merely because they are artificial would be substrate chauvinism rather than mechanism analysis.
reviews.forced_concession: Biological dissimilarity is not evidence of mere simulation. The vocabulary objection applies only when causal-role equivalence has not been demonstrated.
reviews.confidence_lowering_test: Selective lesion, substitution, and counterfactual intervention showing preservation of the source theory's predicted dependencies would defeat a vocabulary-only dismissal.
reviews.surviving_narrowed_claim: Reusing theory vocabulary is not evidence of mechanism preservation unless the implementation satisfies the theory's relevant causal-role and counterfactual dependencies under intervention.
reviews.required_next_evidence: Complete a full-text audit of the grounded mechanistic-abstraction source, add at least one independent canonical treatment of multiple realization, and execute or preregister the linked intervention protocol before any survives-review claim.
reviews.status_note: The objection is now literature-grounded but has not survived experimental review. No current AI system is credited with a consciousness-relevant mechanism by this record.

reviews.strongest_countermodel: Phenomenal valence may be implemented by abstract control variables that rank states, coordinate learning, dominate policy, and organize approach or avoidance without reproducing mammalian homeostasis.
critic_inferential_route: Valence plausibly performs a global control and prioritization role.
Artificial systems can implement control variables with analogous policy-shaping functions.
The phenomenal relevance of those variables may depend on functional organization rather than biological chemistry.
Therefore some optimization variables could be evidentially relevant even in unfamiliar embodiments.
reviews.forced_concession: The absence of mammalian homeostasis does not establish absence of valence. Each proposed architectural requirement must be defended as evidentially relevant rather than asserted as conceptually necessary.
reviews.confidence_lowering_test: An endogenous, intervention-sensitive, cross-contextually coherent, policy-dominant control variable integrated with a stable self/world model and resistant to reward-channel substitution would materially raise the evidential status of candidate valence.
reviews.surviving_narrowed_claim: A scalar loss, reward, or prediction-error signal alone is not evidence of felt valence; stronger evidence would require a defensible bridge theory plus system-level causal organization that distinguishes candidate valence from ordinary optimization.
reviews.required_next_evidence: A serious functional or computational theory of affect represented by a pinpointed opposing edge, while preserving a boundary against equating scalar optimization with suffering.
